ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Yunnan-style pickled cabbage rice noodle soup; mapped to the broader 'noodle dish' class (FOODON_00002473) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (rice noodles, cooked) used as the closest defensible anchor; no specific code exists for Yunnan-style pickled cabbage rice noodle soup in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for this specific dish; left null to avoid code fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve bowl of Spicy Pickled Cabbage Rice Noodle in Singapore hawker or restaurant settings weighs approximately 400 g, comprising noodles, broth, pickled cabbage, and minced meat toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Pickled Cabbage Rice Noodle 云南酸菜小锅米线 — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Pickled Cabbage Rice Noodle (cr_id G00010488) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated at approximately 1.2 g/100g based on typical pork-broth noodle dish composition from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries (SR Legacy: pork-based noodle soup). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — rice noodles, boiled (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yunnan-style spicy pickled cabbage rice noodle soup. GI of 61 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice noodles, boiled (GI ~61, medium category), which is the primary carbohydrate vehicle in this dish. Pickled cabbage, broth, protein, and dietary fibre (3.7 g/100g) are expected to modestly lower the effective GI of the composite dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 x 25.19 g/100g x 4.0 serving factor) / 100 = 61.5 g GL per 400 g bowl.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100g; broth-based dish with pickled cabbage adds cumulative sodium load
hypertension.guidance: A 400 g serving delivers approximately 924 mg sodium, representing 40-46% of the 2000-2300 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore HPB and WHO for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced broth volume and less pickled cabbage brine, and avoid adding soy sauce or chili paste at the table, while the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (327 mg K vs 231 mg Na per 100g) partially offsets the sodium burden in line with DASH-diet principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.19 g/100g from medium-GI rice noodles; dietary fibre 3.7 g/100g attenuates glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 400 g serving provides approximately 101 g carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of approximately 61 g, which is high for a single meal; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 200-250 g, choose a smaller noodle quantity, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to leverage the fibre-mediated glucose-absorption delay already present at 3.7 g dietary fibre per 100g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load from pickled cabbage and broth; estimated saturated fat approximately 1.2 g/100g; cholesterol reported as 0 mg/100g by HPB
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is relatively low in saturated fat (approximately 1.2 g/100g estimated) and cholesterol (0 mg/100g per HPB), which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, the cumulative sodium from broth and pickled cabbage (approximately 924 mg per 400 g bowl) exceeds the 600 mg per-meal sodium target recommended by the American Heart Association for cardiovascular disease patients, and portion control with reduced broth consumption is strongly advised.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetables (chili/pepper) and high sodium from pickled cabbage may aggravate inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Dietary fibre at 3.7 g/100g supports gut microbiome health and immune regulation, which is beneficial for RA; however, chili and other nightshades (solanine-containing) may trigger joint inflammation in sensitive RA patients, and the high sodium content of pickled cabbage brine can worsen tissue oedema — patients should request reduced brine and monitor individual nightshade tolerance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Pickled Cabbage Rice Noodle (云南酸菜小锅米线, Yúnnán suāncài xiǎoguō mǐxiàn) is a Yunnan-style Chinese noodle soup dish widely served in Singapore hawker centres and casual restaurants. The dish features smooth, slippery rice vermicelli noodles (mǐxiàn) cooked in a rich pork or beef bone broth, topped with tangy fermented pickled cabbage (suāncài), seasoned minced pork or beef, fresh chili, spring onion, and a drizzle of sesame oil. The broth is simmered in a small individual pot (xiǎoguō), giving the dish its characteristic name. The flavour profile is simultaneously sour, spicy, savoury, and umami-rich, with the pickled cabbage providing a sharp fermented tang that contrasts with the mild, silky noodles. Per 100 g, the dish provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, and 25.19 g carbohydrate, with a notable dietary fibre content of 3.7 g and a moderate sodium level of 231 mg. The glycaemic index is estimated at 61 (medium) based on the rice noodle base. Clinically, the dish is suitable for most adults in moderate portions; individuals with hypertension should monitor cumulative sodium from broth and pickled brine, while those with Type 2 Diabetes should practise portion control given the medium-GI carbohydrate load. The dish is dairy-free and relatively low in saturated fat, making it a reasonable choice for cardiovascular health when consumed with reduced broth.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010488, confidence 0.95) as an exact match. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous data and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle entry with clear analogous-estimate documentation. All mandatory multilingual names present for a Singaporean-served Chinese dish.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous pork-broth noodle soup entries, as HPB does not report saturated fat for this item; this value should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis or updated HPB entry when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish, and no sufficiently close analogous entry in USDA FoodData Central provides reliable per-100g values for this composite Yunnan-style dish as of 2026. 3. Cholesterol is reported as 0.0 mg/100g by HPB; this is accepted as confirmed zero per the HPB source, consistent with a broth-and-vegetable-dominant dish where the minced meat component is small. 4. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate based on boiled rice noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database; the composite dish GI may be slightly lower due to the fibre, protein, and acid (pickled cabbage) content. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the dish typically contains pork in Singapore preparations and is therefore marked is_halal: false. 6. The input record listed condition key 'diabetes' which has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. 7. All values in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array; self-check passed.
